column4: Mass protests have erupted against government austerity measures.
Stay the course on fiscal discipline despite public anger.
Partially reverse the cuts to calm public unrest.
A major bank is on the verge of collapse threatening the entire financial system.
Use public funds to rescue the failing bank and stabilize the system.
Allow the bank to collapse and let the market sort itself out.
A government bond auction has failed to attract sufficient bids.
Have the central bank step in to buy unsold bonds.
Offer bonds at higher yields to attract private buyers.
Wealthy investors are moving capital out of the country amid uncertainty.
Restrict the flow of money out of the country.
Announce investor-friendly policies to restore confidence.
The central bank governor term is expiring. You must choose a successor.
Choose a governor focused on fighting inflation with higher rates.
Choose a governor who favors low rates to support growth.
A sudden spike in commodity prices is driving up costs across the economy.
Provide subsidies to shield consumers from rising prices.
Allow market prices to rise naturally.
The national currency is in freefall as markets lose confidence.
Dramatically increase interest rates to defend the currency.
Accept currency weakness and focus on export competitiveness.
Prices are falling across the economy. Consumers are delaying purchases.
Launch a major government spending program to boost demand.
Flood the economy with money to push prices back up.
Experts propose launching a central bank digital currency.
Create and launch a government-backed digital currency.
Maintain the current monetary system without digital currency.
Banks holding government debt are losing value as bond prices fall.
Inject public funds into banks to break the doom loop.
Require banks to write down losses and restructure.
The European Central Bank is meeting to decide interest rates.
Lobby for lower rates to support your economy.
Support the current monetary stance.
A proposal for jointly-issued European bonds is being debated.
Back the proposal for shared European debt instruments.
Reject shared debt as a threat to fiscal sovereignty.
Politicians are calling for a referendum on leaving the eurozone.
Firmly commit to staying in the eurozone.
Let the public debate proceed on eurozone membership.
Automatic spending cuts and tax increases are about to take effect.
Cancel the automatic measures and increase spending.
Allow the fiscal tightening to proceed.
Foreign creditors are demanding repayment but the treasury cannot meet obligations.
Seek to restructure debt terms with creditors.
Cut domestic spending to honor foreign debt obligations.
Advisors propose issuing green bonds to fund environmental projects.
Launch a green bond program to fund sustainable investment.
Focus on conventional financing methods.
Property prices are surging far beyond what fundamentals justify.
Impose strict mortgage lending requirements to cool the market.
Take a wait-and-see approach to the property market.
The IMF is offering a bailout package with strict conditions attached.
Take the bailout with its austerity conditions.
Refuse IMF assistance and seek alternative solutions.
Economists are debating whether the inflation target should be raised.
Increase the inflation target to give more room for monetary policy.
Keep the current inflation target to preserve credibility.
A devastating natural disaster requires massive government spending.
Launch comprehensive disaster relief funded by emergency borrowing.
Provide basic assistance while minimizing fiscal impact.
The pension system faces a growing funding shortfall.
Increase the retirement age to reduce pension costs.
Raise pension contribution rates for current workers.
Campaigners demand full transparency on government borrowing.
Publish comprehensive debt data openly.
Release only summary information on public finances.
A credit rating agency is threatening to downgrade the sovereign debt rating.
Announce urgent fiscal measures to prevent the downgrade.
Publicly dispute the methodology and maintain current policy.
Advisors propose creating a sovereign wealth fund from budget surpluses.
Establish a sovereign wealth fund for long-term investment.
Use surpluses for tax cuts or direct payments instead.
The economy faces rising inflation and rising unemployment simultaneously.
Tighten monetary policy to control prices at the cost of jobs.
Stimulate the economy to create jobs at the risk of more inflation.
Foreign capital inflows have abruptly reversed leaving a financing gap.
Sharply raise interest rates to attract capital back.
Approach international institutions for emergency financing.
Evidence emerges of massive capital being hidden in offshore tax havens.
Launch aggressive enforcement against offshore tax evasion.
Offer a tax amnesty to encourage voluntary repatriation of funds.
A major trading partner is threatening retaliatory tariffs on your exports.
Impose counter-tariffs to defend domestic industries.
Seek diplomatic resolution to avoid escalation.
The banking crisis is spreading across the eurozone as interconnected institutions face cascading losses.
Push for emergency ECB intervention to contain the contagion.
Protect national institutions by isolating them from eurozone exposure.
Work with eurozone partners on a joint fiscal rescue package.
The banking crisis is spreading as interconnected institutions face cascading losses.
Order the central bank to flood the system with emergency liquidity.
Protect national institutions by isolating them from foreign exposure.
Issue a blanket government guarantee on all bank deposits.
Investors are rapidly pulling capital out of the country amid a eurozone confidence crisis.
Request emergency ECB measures to stabilize capital flows.
Signal fiscal discipline to reassure investors.
Allow markets to adjust without intervention.
Investors are rapidly pulling capital out of the country amid economic uncertainty.
Restrict the movement of money out of the country.
Sharply raise interest rates to make the currency attractive.
Allow markets to adjust without intervention.
Rating agencies have placed the sovereign debt on negative watch for a potential downgrade.
Announce austerity measures to prevent the downgrade.
Present a credible medium-term fiscal plan to the agencies.
Publicly challenge the rating methodology and maintain course.
The euro is under severe pressure due to concerns about your country's fiscal position.
Demonstrate fiscal commitment to stabilize eurozone confidence.
Push for joint intervention to defend the currency.
Allow the currency adjustment to boost exports.
The national currency is collapsing as markets lose confidence.
Dramatically raise interest rates to defend the currency.
Use foreign reserves to buy the domestic currency.
Let the currency find its new level and focus on exports.
A large tranche of government debt is maturing and must be refinanced.
Lock in rates with 10-year bonds despite higher yields.
Use cheaper short-term financing and hope rates fall.
Split the rollover across short and long maturities.
The finance ministry proposes officially declaring government bonds as risk-free assets.
Officially designate sovereign debt as zero-risk for regulatory purposes.
Maintain market-based risk assessment of government debt.
Deflation is deepening across the eurozone threatening a lost decade.
Lobby the ECB for aggressive monetary easing.
Launch domestic spending programs to fight deflation.
Focus on supply-side reforms to boost long-term competitiveness.
Deflation is deepening and consumers are delaying spending in anticipation of lower prices.
Order the central bank to flood the economy with money.
Launch a massive government spending program.
Push interest rates below zero to discourage saving.
The sovereign-bank doom loop is intensifying as losses cascade through the eurozone.
Apply for European Stability Mechanism emergency funding.
Require banks to absorb losses and recapitalize.
Declare fiscal emergency and implement drastic budget cuts.
The sovereign-bank doom loop is intensifying as government debt losses cascade through banks.
Order the central bank to buy government bonds to stop the spiral.
Require banks to raise new capital and absorb losses.
Restructure government debt to reduce the burden on banks.
A government bond auction has failed in a eurozone member state.
Reissue bonds at significantly higher yields to attract buyers.
Ask the ECB to purchase bonds in the secondary market.
Scale back borrowing plans and cut spending immediately.
A government bond auction has failed to attract sufficient bids.
Reissue bonds at significantly higher yields to attract buyers.
Have the central bank directly purchase the unsold bonds.
Scale back borrowing plans and cut spending immediately.
The economy is in recession and the fiscal rule is constraining the government's ability to respond.
Temporarily suspend fiscal discipline to allow emergency spending.
Adjust the fiscal rule to allow cyclical flexibility.
Keep fiscal discipline despite the recession.
The economy is in severe crisis and the IMF is offering a comprehensive stabilization program.
Accept IMF conditions including structural reforms and austerity.
Refuse external intervention and attempt domestic solutions.
Soaring prices have triggered widespread civil unrest and rioting.
Impose emergency price controls on essential goods.
Order drastic interest rate increases to crush inflation.
Provide direct cash transfers to the hardest-hit households.
A major trading partner has sharply devalued their currency making your exports uncompetitive.
Devalue the domestic currency to match.
Impose temporary tariffs on imports from the devaluing country.
Accept reduced competitiveness and let the market adjust.
Advisors propose using fiscal surpluses to establish a national wealth fund.
Create a sovereign wealth fund invested in global assets.
Use surpluses to reduce the national debt instead.
Distribute surpluses as tax cuts or direct payments.
The eurozone economy faces simultaneous high inflation and rising unemployment.
Push for ECB tightening to control prices at the cost of jobs.
Lobby for looser ECB policy to support growth.
Focus on structural reforms to address both problems simultaneously.
The economy faces simultaneous high inflation and rising unemployment.
Tighten monetary policy aggressively to control prices.
Cut rates and stimulate demand to create jobs.
Focus on structural reforms to address both problems simultaneously.
A major state-owned enterprise is on the verge of collapse threatening thousands of jobs.
Inject public funds to keep the enterprise operational.
Provide conditional support with mandatory restructuring.
Allow the enterprise to collapse and redirect support to displaced workers.
Major technology companies are offering to relocate if given a multi-year tax holiday.
Offer generous tax breaks to attract tech investment and jobs.
Offer moderate incentives tied to job creation targets.
Refuse special treatment and maintain a level tax playing field.
The yield curve has inverted signaling a potential recession ahead.
Launch fiscal and monetary stimulus to prevent the recession.
Hold steady and monitor the situation before acting.
